license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.88ahzse
title: Enrichment of a specific polyadenylated RNA for nanopore direct RNA sequencing (RNA SPACE)
author: Paul MK Gordon

steps_raw.instruction: Have the three oligos synthesized (RE oligo, RTA Oligos A & B), by designing your own using the open source software and your reference data. RNA SPACE Paul Gordon

In the same tube, perform 3h or overnight restriction digest according to the NEB protocol for your enzyme, e.g. http://nebcloner.neb.com/#!/protocol/re/single/Taq1 (65C for Taq1, 37C for the other 5 restriction enzymes) The longer incubation is suggested due to the lower efficiency of RNA:DNA restriction compared to DNA:DNA. Step 5 can be performed while you wait for the incubation to finish.
In a 1.5 ml tube, add up to 2µg of your total RNA (in 10µl or less), and 1µl of 1µM RE oligo.
Heat the incubation tube to 80º C for 2 min and let it cool down slowly (0.1º C/sec).

If this is the first time using these custom oligos: In a new tube, anneal standard RTA oligo A and custom RTA oligo B 1:1 at 1.4 µM in buffer (10 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.5, 50 mM NaCl) by heating to 95º C for 2 min and letting them cool down slowly (0.1º C/sec). Otherwise: fetch the custom oligo duplex left over from a previous run.
After the 3 hour incubation has finished, perform the RNeasy MinElute Cleanup Kit protocol (spin column) on the contents of the NEB enzyme reaction tube.

Perform the Oxford Nanopore Technologies Direct RNA sequencing protocol (RNA-002 kit) on the RNA isolated by MinElute. https://community.nanoporetech.com/protocols/ss-direct-rna-sequencing-sqk-rna002/checklist.pdf?devices=minion
Add 1ul of the RTA duplex to the tube of eluted RNA. Store any remainder of the RTA duplex, for future uses.
